summary: Fix 3 broken sprint-simulation scorers: finalQuality, cumulativeRework, contextRecovery
context: Run 73972189 analysis revealed: finalQuality always 100 (zero variance), cumulativeRework only 2.7pt spread across conditions, contextRecovery always 100 for Twining conditions. Root cause: test results computed AFTER scoring, weak heuristics, process-only measurement.
rationale: 1) Moved test execution before scoring in orchestrator so scorers get real compilation/test data via RawResults.testResults. 2) finalQuality now uses 5 weighted signals (compilation, test pass rate, component verification, architecture consistency, multi-channel integration) instead of binary file-existence checks. 3) cumulativeRework uses linear 6x amplifier instead of flat cubic curve, filters coordination files from overlap count, removes overlap penalty cap. 4) contextRecovery mixes process (40pts) + efficiency ratio (35pts) + time-to-first-write (25pts) instead of binary 100 for any Twining tool usage.

alternatives.option: Use LLM-as-judge for all dimensions instead of automated scorers
alternatives.reason_rejected: Adds cost and latency per iteration; automated scorers are faster and more reproducible. LLM judge already exists as optional path for finalQuality.

alternatives.option: Keep cubic curve for cumulativeRework but raise overlap cap
alternatives.reason_rejected: Cubic curve is fundamentally too flat in the 0-5% rework range where all conditions cluster. Linear with amplifier gives better spread.

assumptions: Rework ratios will continue to cluster in 0-5% range across conditions
12-session sprint produces 15-25 cross-session file overlaps on source code (after filtering coordination files)
6x rework amplifier and 1.5pts/overlap give ~10-15pt spread across conditions
